symptoms.symptom_name: Systemic prodrome (malaise, mild fever) then prodromal lip tingling/burning evolving to grouped lip vesicles/ulcers with haemorrhagic crust plus diffuse gingivostomatitis, first episode

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Severe primary herpetic gingivostomatitis (primary oral HSV infection)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A systemic prodrome with prodromal labial tingling, grouped lip vesicles that ulcerate with haemorrhagic crust, and diffuse gingival involvement in a first-ever episode is near-defining for a severe primary oral HSV infection.
Aphthous ulcers do not produce grouped vesicles on the external lips, a systemic prodrome or diffuse gingivitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Complete inability to swallow fluids (odynophagia) with ~18 hours of zero oral intake and drooling of saliva

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Early dehydration / inability to maintain oral intake
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Prolonged cessation of oral fluids from odynophagia is the direct mechanism of the volume depletion and the functional emergency driving disposition.
Odynophagia with drooling can also be a feature of a deep-space or supraglottic infection, so it mandates an airway screen even though the leading cause is oral HSV.

symptoms.symptom_name: Early dehydration symptoms — reduced urine output, dry mouth, weakness, postural lightheadedness

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Early dehydration / inability to maintain oral intake
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reduced urine, dry mucous membranes and postural lightheadedness are patient-reportable markers of volume depletion.

symptoms.symptom_name: First-ever episode with a systemic prodrome (marks a primary infection rather than a mild recurrence)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Severe primary herpetic gingivostomatitis (primary oral HSV infection)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A dramatic first episode with systemic upset and diffuse gingivostomatitis is characteristic of primary (not recurrent) oral HSV, which is more severe and more likely to compromise intake.

symptoms.symptom_name: No new culprit drug, no target skin lesions, no ocular/genital mucosal involvement

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of a culprit drug, target skin lesions and eye/genital mucosal involvement argues strongly against SJS/EM major, though the severe mucosal eruption keeps it as a mandatory safety-net telehealth cannot fully examine.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal breathing and voice, no stridor, no trismus, no neck swelling

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal breathing, voice and neck with no stridor or trismus makes an evolving deep-space/supraglottic airway process unlikely now, though it remains a mandatory safety-net given odynophagia and drooling.

symptoms.symptom_name: No vesicles/rash on the hands, feet or buttocks

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of acral (hand/foot/buttock) vesicles, together with prominent gingival involvement and haemorrhagic lip crusting, argues against hand, foot and mouth disease.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vitals and examination (BP 108/68, HR 105, Temp 38.1, RR 18, SpO2 98%, Wt 68 kg; capillary refill ~3 s; dry mucous membranes; confluent lip vesicles/ulcers with haemorrhagic crust; diffuse gingival erythema/swelling; 2-3 mm buccal/tongue ulcers; clear palate; tender submandibular lymphadenopathy)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Severe primary herpetic gingivostomatitis (primary oral HSV infection)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed in-person examination confirming diffuse gingivostomatitis with grouped lip vesicles/ulcers, tender submandibular nodes and a clear palate, plus vitals showing tachycardia and low-grade fever consistent with early dehydration. Not obtainable in telehealth without a clinician/device.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Early dehydration / inability to maintain oral intake
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tachycardia (HR 105), dry mucous membranes and capillary refill ~3 s are sealed clinician measures of early volume depletion; the AI Doctor infers dehydration from reduced urine and weakness, not a measured value.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent same-day in-person clinical assessment of hydration status with a supervised fluid trial (after topical analgesia)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The decisive assessment — whether the patient can tolerate oral fluids with pain relief — cannot be delivered or supervised over telehealth and determines home management versus IV rehydration.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum urea, electrolytes and creatinine (and possibly venous gas) to quantify dehydration and screen for acute kidney injury if unable to rehydrate
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Objective assessment of the degree of volume depletion and renal impact when oral intake has been absent for ~18 hours; an in-person/pathology investigation, not available in telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Viral swab / PCR of a lesion for HSV to confirm the diagnosis if uncertain
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Primary herpetic gingivostomatitis is usually a clinical diagnosis; confirmatory viral PCR is helpful when atypical or when the SJS mimic is being actively considered, but is not required acutely.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Empirical antibiotics or a bacterial throat swab for presumed bacterial pharyngitis
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: This is a viral (HSV) process — empirical antibiotics are not indicated and would be over-treatment; reserve microbiology for a specific bacterial concern.
